transport.c(3) Library Functions Manual transport.c(3)

NAME

transport.c - WebSocket transport implementation for ACIP protocol.

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING 64
Maximum incoming message queue size (frames received from peer). #define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING 512
Maximum outgoing message queue size (frames to send to peer). #define WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING
#define WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING

Functions


acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create (const char *name, const char *url, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket client transport. acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create (const char *name, struct lws *wsi, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection.

Detailed Description

WebSocket transport implementation for ACIP protocol.

Implements the acip_transport_t interface for WebSocket connections. Enables browser clients to connect via WebSocket protocol.

ARCHITECTURE:

  • Uses libwebsockets for WebSocket protocol handling
  • Async libwebsockets callbacks bridge to sync recv() via ringbuffer
  • Thread-safe receive queue handles async message arrival
  • Same pattern as WebRTC transport for consistency

MESSAGE FLOW:

1.
send(): Synchronous write via lws_write()
2.
LWS callback: Async write to receive ringbuffer
3.
recv(): Blocking read from receive ringbuffer

MEMORY OWNERSHIP:

  • Transport OWNS wsi (WebSocket instance)
  • Receive queue owns buffered message data
  • recv() allocates message buffer, caller must free

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

Definition in file websocket/transport.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING 64

Maximum incoming message queue size (frames received from peer). Set to 64 for balance: allows buffering across one large frame's fragments while keeping latency reasonable. With ~70 fragments per 291KB frame, we need at least 64 slots to avoid dropping the frame while it's fragmenting.

Definition at line 57 of file websocket/transport.c.

#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING 512

Maximum outgoing message queue size (frames to send to peer). Set to 64 to match incoming queue and prevent "BINARY after FIN" errors. Large video frames fragment into ~70 chunks; queue must be large enough to buffer one complete message worth of fragments.

Definition at line 66 of file websocket/transport.c.

#define WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING

Definition at line 69 of file websocket/transport.c.

Referenced by acip_websocket_client_transport_create(), and acip_websocket_server_transport_create().

#define WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING

Definition at line 70 of file websocket/transport.c.

Referenced by acip_websocket_client_transport_create(), and acip_websocket_server_transport_create().

Function Documentation

acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create (const char * name, const char * url, crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx)

Create WebSocket client transport.

Parameters

url WebSocket URL (e.g., "ws://localhost:27225")
crypto_ctx Optional encryption context (can be NULL)

Returns

Transport instance or NULL on failure

Definition at line 1350 of file websocket/transport.c.

1351                                                                                        {
1352   if (!name) {
1353     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1354     return NULL;
1355   }
1356 
1357   if (!url) {
1358     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "url is required");
1359     return NULL;
1360   }
1361 
1362   // Parse URL to extract host, port, and path
1363   // Format: ws://host:port/path or wss://host:port/path
1364   const char *protocol_end = strstr(url, "://");
1365   if (!protocol_end) {
1366     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid WebSocket URL format (missing ://)");
1367     return NULL;
1368   }
1369 
1370   bool use_ssl = (strncmp(url, "wss://", 6) == 0);
1371   const char *host_start = protocol_end + 3;
1372 
1373   // Find port (if specified)
1374   const char *port_start = strchr(host_start, ':');
1375   const char *path_start = strchr(host_start, '/');
1376 
1377   char host[256] = {0};
1378   int port = use_ssl ? 443 : 27226; // Default: wss:// uses 443, ws:// uses 27226 (ascii-chat WebSocket port)
1379   char path[256] = "/";
1380 
1381   if (port_start && (!path_start || port_start < path_start)) {
1382     // Port is specified
1383     size_t host_len = port_start - host_start;
1384     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1385       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1386       return NULL;
1387     }
1388     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1389     host[host_len] = '\0';
1390 
1391     // Extract port
1392     port = atoi(port_start + 1);
1393     if (port <= 0 || port > 65535) {
1394       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid port number");
1395       return NULL;
1396     }
1397   } else {
1398     // No port specified, use default
1399     size_t host_len = path_start ? (size_t)(path_start - host_start) : strlen(host_start);
1400     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1401       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1402       return NULL;
1403     }
1404     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1405     host[host_len] = '\0';
1406   }
1407 
1408   // Extract path
1409   if (path_start) {
1410     SAFE_STRNCPY(path, path_start, sizeof(path) - 1);
1411     path[sizeof(path) - 1] = '\0';
1412   }
1413 
1414   log_info("Connecting to WebSocket: %s (host=%s, port=%d, path=%s, ssl=%d)", url, host, port, path, use_ssl);
1415 
1416   // Allocate transport structure
1417   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1418   if (!transport) {
1419     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1420     return NULL;
1421   }
1422 
1423   // Allocate WebSocket-specific data
1424   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1425       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1426   if (!ws_data) {
1427     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1428     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1429     return NULL;
1430   }
1431 
1432   // Initialize destruction state flag (CALLOC zero-initializes, but explicit for clarity)
1433   atomic_store_bool(&ws_data->is_destroying, false);
1434 
1435   // Create receive queue
1436   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1437   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1438     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1439     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1440     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1441     return NULL;
1442   }
1443 
1444   // Create send queue for client transport to buffer outgoing messages
1445   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1446   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1447     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1448     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1449     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1450     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1451     return NULL;
1452   }
1453 
1454   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1455   char recv_name[64], state_name[64];
1456   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1457   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1458 
1459   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1460     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1461     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1462     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1463     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1464     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1465     return NULL;
1466   }
1467 
1468   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1469     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1470     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1471     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1472     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1473     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1474     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1475     return NULL;
1476   }
1477 
1478   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1479     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1480     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1481     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1482     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1483     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1484     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1485     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1486     return NULL;
1487   }
1488 
1489   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1490     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1491     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1492     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1493     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1494     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1495     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1496     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1497     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1498     return NULL;
1499   }
1500 
1501   // Allocate initial send buffer
1502   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = LWS_PRE + 524288; // Match tx_packet_size (512KB)
1503   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(ws_data->send_buffer_capacity, uint8_t *);
1504   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1505     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1506     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1507     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1508     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1509     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1510     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1511     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1512     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1513     return NULL;
1514   }
1515 
1516   // Create libwebsockets context
1517   // Protocol array must persist for lifetime of context - use static
1518   // NOTE: Define ONLY "acip" protocol for WebSocket connections
1519   // libwebsockets provides built-in "h1" and "http" protocols for WebSocket upgrade handshake.
1520   // When local_protocol_name="h1" and protocol="acip" are specified:
1521   // 1. libwebsockets uses built-in h1 (HTTP/1.1) for initial connection
1522   // 2. HTTP upgrade happens automatically (101 Switching Protocols)
1523   // 3. Connection switches to "acip" protocol using our custom callback
1524   static struct lws_protocols client_protocols[] = {
1525       {
1526           "acip", // ACIP protocol - used after WebSocket upgrade handshake completes
1527           websocket_callback,
1528           0,      // Per-session data - using connect_info.userdata instead
1529           524288, // RX buffer size
1530           0,      // ID
1531           NULL,   // User pointer (set from connect_info.userdata)
1532           524288  // TX packet size
1533       },
1534       {NULL, NULL, 0, 0, 0, NULL, 0} // Terminator
1535   };
1536 
1537   // Disable client compression for now - causes assertion in lws_set_extension_option()
1538   // This is a known issue with libwebsockets permessage-deflate negotiation
1539   // Server-side compression is still enabled, data will be compressed from server to client
1540   // but client->server traffic remains uncompressed (acceptable since client sends less data)
1541   // static const struct lws_extension client_extensions[] = {
1542   //     {"permessage-deflate", lws_extension_callback_pm_deflate, "permessage-deflate; client_max_window_bits=15"},
1543   //     {NULL, NULL, NULL}};
1544 
1545   struct lws_context_creation_info info;
1546   memset(&info, 0, sizeof(info));
1547   info.port = CONTEXT_PORT_NO_LISTEN; // Client mode - no listening
1548   info.protocols = client_protocols;
1549   info.gid = (gid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1550   info.uid = (uid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1551   // Initialize SSL globally for client (required even for clients to use OpenSSL)
1552   // The LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT flag works for both servers and clients
1553   info.options = LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT;
1554   info.extensions = NULL; // Disable client compression due to lws_set_extension_option() assertion
1555 
1556   // Increase per-thread service buffer to prevent fragmentation of large messages
1557   // Default is 4KB, causing 291KB frames to fragment into 73 × 4KB chunks
1558   // Increase to 512KB to match server and allow larger WebSocket frames without fragmentation
1559   info.pt_serv_buf_size = 512 * 1024; // 512KB per-thread service buffer
1560 
1561   // Configure keep-alive to prevent idle disconnects during handshake
1562   // PING every 30 seconds if idle, close after 35 seconds total with no response
1563   static const lws_retry_bo_t client_keep_alive_policy = {
1564       .secs_since_valid_ping = 30,   // Send PING after 30s idle
1565       .secs_since_valid_hangup = 35, // Hangup if still idle after 35s
1566   };
1567   info.retry_and_idle_policy = &client_keep_alive_policy;
1568 
1569   // Enable libwebsockets logging through centralized logging system
1570   lws_log_init_client();
1571 
1572   ws_data->context = lws_create_context(&info);
1573 
1574   if (!ws_data->context) {
1575     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1576     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1577     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1578     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1579     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1580     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1581     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1582     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1583     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1584     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create libwebsockets context");
1585     return NULL;
1586   }
1587 
1588   // Initialize connection state flags BEFORE calling lws_client_connect_via_info
1589   // The callback might fire immediately, so all fields must be ready
1590   ws_data->is_connected = false;      // Will be set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_ESTABLISHED
1591   ws_data->connection_failed = false; // Set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_CONNECTION_ERROR
1592   ws_data->owns_context = true;       // Client transport owns the context
1593 
1594   // Connect to WebSocket server
1595   log_debug("Initiating WebSocket connection to %s:%d%s", host, port, path);
1596   struct lws_client_connect_info connect_info;
1597   memset(&connect_info, 0, sizeof(connect_info));
1598   connect_info.context = ws_data->context;
1599   connect_info.address = host;
1600   connect_info.port = port;
1601   connect_info.path = path;
1602   connect_info.host = host;
1603   connect_info.origin = host;
1604   // Use h1 (HTTP/1.1) protocol for WebSocket upgrade with acip as the subprotocol
1605   // - local_protocol_name="h1": libwebsockets' built-in HTTP/1.1 handler for WebSocket upgrade
1606   // - protocol="acip": Request this subprotocol from the remote server (Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header)
1607   // libwebsockets internally uses h1 regardless of the protocol name, so we use h1 directly
1608   connect_info.local_protocol_name = "h1";  // libwebsockets built-in HTTP/1.1
1609   connect_info.protocol = "acip";  // Remote subprotocol to request from server
1610   // Use SSL + skip server certificate hostname verification + allow self-signed certs (for development)
1611   connect_info.ssl_connection = use_ssl ? (LCCSCF_USE_SSL | LCCSCF_SKIP_SERVER_CERT_HOSTNAME_CHECK | LCCSCF_ALLOW_SELFSIGNED) : 0;
1612   connect_info.userdata = ws_data;
1613 
1614   log_debug("Calling lws_client_connect_via_info...");
1615   ws_data->wsi = lws_client_connect_via_info(&connect_info);
1616   log_debug("lws_client_connect_via_info returned: %p", (void *)ws_data->wsi);
1617   if (!ws_data->wsi) {
1618     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1619     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1620     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1621     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1622     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1623     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1624     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1625     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1626     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1627     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1628     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to connect to WebSocket server");
1629     return NULL;
1630   }
1631 
1632   // Initialize transport
1633   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1634   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1635   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1636 
1637   // Start service thread immediately - it will drive TLS handshake with frequent polling
1638   // The service thread checks is_destroying and context validity before calling lws_service,
1639   // so it's safe to start it right away without delays.
1640   ws_data->service_running = true;
1641   if (asciichat_thread_create(&ws_data->service_thread, "ws_service", websocket_service_thread, ws_data) != 0) {
1642     log_error("Failed to create WebSocket service thread");
1643     ws_data->service_running = false;
1644     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1645     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1646     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1647     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1648     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1649     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1650     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1651     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1652     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1653     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1654     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to create service thread");
1655     return NULL;
1656   }
1657   log_debug("WebSocket service thread started");
1658 
1659   // CRITICAL FIX: Do NOT block the main thread waiting for connection!
1660   // This prevents stdin/keyboard input from being processed and causes the client to hang.
1661   // The service thread will establish the connection asynchronously.
1662   // The protocol layer (recv) will detect if connection fails and handle it there.
1663   // Return immediately so the main thread can respond to keyboard input.
1664   log_debug("WebSocket transport created, service thread will establish connection asynchronously");
1665 
1666   // Register transport
1667   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1668 
1669   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1670   NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1671 
1672   // Return transport immediately - connection will be established by service thread
1673   // If connection fails, recv() will detect is_connected=false and return error
1674   return transport;
1675 }




References asciichat_thread_create(),
    atomic_store_bool(), lws_log_init_client(),
    mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(), ringbuffer_create(),
    ringbuffer_destroy(), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, and
    WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE.


Referenced by server_connection_establish(), and
    websocket_client_connect().






acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create
  (const char * name, struct lws * wsi, crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx)


Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection. Wraps
    an already-established libwebsockets connection (from server accept). Used
    by websocket_server module to create transports for incoming clients.




Parameters


wsi Established libwebsockets connection (not
  owned by transport)


crypto_ctx Optional crypto context


Returns


Transport instance or NULL on error


Definition at line 1687 of file
    websocket/transport.c.


1688                                                                                        {
1689   if (!name) {
1690     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1691     return NULL;
1692   }
1693 
1694   if (!wsi) {
1695     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid wsi parameter");
1696     return NULL;
1697   }
1698 
1699   // Allocate transport structure
1700   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1701   if (!transport) {
1702     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1703     return NULL;
1704   }
1705 
1706   // Allocate transport-specific data
1707   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1708       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1709   if (!ws_data) {
1710     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1711     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1712     return NULL;
1713   }
1714 
1715   // Initialize receive queue
1716   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1717   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1718     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1719     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1720     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1721     return NULL;
1722   }
1723 
1724   // Initialize send queue (for server-side transports)
1725   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1726   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1727     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1728     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1729     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1730     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1731     return NULL;
1732   }
1733 
1734   // Initialize pending-free queue for deferred buffer freeing
1735   // permessage-deflate compression holds buffer references asynchronously,
1736   // so we defer freeing to prevent use-after-free errors
1737   ws_data->pending_free_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(pending_free_item_t), 256);
1738   if (!ws_data->pending_free_queue) {
1739     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1740     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1741     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1742     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1743     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create pending-free queue");
1744     return NULL;
1745   }
1746 
1747   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1748   char recv_name[64], send_name[64], state_name[64], pending_free_name[64];
1749   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1750   snprintf(send_name, sizeof(send_name), "send_%s", name);
1751   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1752   snprintf(pending_free_name, sizeof(pending_free_name), "pending_free_%s", name);
1753 
1754   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1755     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1756     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1757     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1758     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1759     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1760     return NULL;
1761   }
1762 
1763   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1764     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1765     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1766     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1767     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1768     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1769     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1770     return NULL;
1771   }
1772 
1773   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->send_mutex, send_name) != 0) {
1774     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1775     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1776     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1777     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1778     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1779     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1780     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize send mutex");
1781     return NULL;
1782   }
1783 
1784   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1785     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1786     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1787     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1788     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1789     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1790     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1791     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1792     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1793     return NULL;
1794   }
1795 
1796   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1797     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1798     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1799     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1800     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1801     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1802     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1803     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1804     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1805     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1806     return NULL;
1807   }
1808 
1809   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->pending_free_mutex, pending_free_name) != 0) {
1810     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1811     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1812     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1813     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1814     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1815     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1816     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1817     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->pending_free_queue);
1818     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1819     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1820     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize pending-free mutex");
1821     return NULL;
1822   }
1823 
1824   // Allocate send buffer with LWS_PRE padding
1825   size_t initial_capacity = 4096 + LWS_PRE;
1826   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(initial_capacity, uint8_t *);
1827   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1828     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1829     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1830     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1831     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1832     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1833     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1834     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1835     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1836     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1837     return NULL;
1838   }
1839   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = initial_capacity;
1840 
1841   // Store connection info (server-side: no context ownership, connection already established)
1842   ws_data->wsi = wsi;
1843   ws_data->context = lws_get_context(wsi); // Get context from wsi (not owned)
1844   ws_data->owns_context = false;           // Server owns context, not transport
1845   ws_data->is_connected = true;            // Already connected (server-side)
1846   log_info("[WEBSOCKET_TRANSPORT_CREATE] ★★★ SERVER TRANSPORT CREATED: is_connected=true, wsi=%p, ws_data=%p",
1847            (void *)wsi, (void *)ws_data);
1848   log_debug("Server transport created: is_connected=true, wsi=%p", (void *)wsi);
1849 
1850   // Initialize transport
1851   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1852   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1853   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1854 
1855   log_info("Created WebSocket server transport (crypto: %s)", crypto_ctx ? "enabled" : "disabled");
1856 
1857   // Register transport
1858   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1859 
1860   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1861   NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1862 
1863   return transport;
1864 }






References mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(),
    ringbuffer_create(), ringbuffer_destroy(),
    WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, and WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE.








Author


Generated automatically by Doxygen for ascii-chat from the source
    code.







  

    Version 0.9.0
    ascii-chat
